channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: North Korea accuses South Korea of flying drones to its capital and threatens to attack next time
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea has accused rival South Korea of flying drones to its capital to drop anti-North Korean propaganda leaflets and threatened to respond with force if such flights occur again. South Korea issued a vague denial of the allegation.
item.dc:creator: KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Russia launches 4th aerial attack in a week against Ukraine's grain-exporting Odesa region
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — A nighttime Russian missile strike on Odesa killed at least four people including a 16-year-old girl, regional authorities said Friday, in the latest in a series of attacks this week on the southern Ukrainian region that are likely intended to disrupt the country’s grain exports
item.dc:creator: HANNA ARHIROVA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Nobel Peace Prize given to Japanese organization Nihon Hidankyo for its work against nuclear weapons
item.description: The Nobel Peace Prize was awarded Friday to Nihon Hidankyo, a Japanese organization of survivors of the U.S. atomic bombings of Hiroshima and Nagasaki, for its activism against nuclear weapons.
item.dc:creator: MIKE CORDER and ELENA BECATOROS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ukraine's Zelenskyy meets Pope Francis as he continues his European tour to push his 'victory plan'
item.description: VATICAN CITY (AP) — Pope Francis met Volodymyr Zelenskyy at the Vatican on Friday, the latest step on the Ukrainian president's European tour to win support for his proposed “victory plan” aimed at ending the war with Russia. The pope and the Ukrainian leader held private talks for 35 minutes.
item.dc:creator: GIADA ZAMPANO, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russia's Putin begins a visit to Turkmenistan for forum with regional leaders, including Iran
item.description: ASHGABAT, Turkmenistan (AP) — Russian President Vladimir Putin met Iran’s president on Friday at a time when Tehran is supplying weapons for Moscow’s war in Ukraine and concerns are growing over escalating attacks between Israel and Iran and its militant allies.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Blinken tells ASEAN the US is worried about China's 'dangerous' actions in disputed sea.
item.description: U.S. Secretary of State Antony Blinken told Southeast Asian leaders Friday that the U.S. is concerned about China’s “increasingly dangerous and unlawful” activities in the disputed South China Sea during an annual summit meeting, and pledged the U.S.
item.dc:creator: Eileen Ng And Jintamas Saksornchai, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: New Zealand ship didn’t sink because its captain was a woman, the ‘appalled’ defense minister says
item.description: New Zealand’s Defense Minister Judith Collins has decried what she called misogynistic online comments about the woman commander of a navy ship which ran aground, caught fire and sank off the coast of Samoa on Sunday.
item.dc:creator: Charlotte Graham-Mclay, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Brown votes against student proposal to divest from companies allegedly connected to war in Gaza
item.description: Brown University announced Thursday it will not divest from companies that pro-Palestinian students say contribute to Israel's war efforts in Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Lexi Lonas Cochran, The Hill

item.title: UN-backed experts say Israel is destroying Gaza's health sector and both sides have tortured people
item.description: A U.N. commission on Thursday accused Israel of destroying Gaza's health care system through “relentless and deliberate attacks” in its yearlong war with Hamas and said that Palestinian prisoners and Israeli hostages have been tortured and sexually abused.
item.dc:creator: JOSEPH KRAUSS, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Colombia’s river guardians battle to protect the Atrato amid threats and abandonment
item.description: PAIMADO, Colombia (AP) — Sediment and pebbles are all that’s left on the earth around much of Bernardino Mosquera’s small riverside community in northwest Colombia’s Choco region.
item.dc:creator: STEVEN GRATTAN, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Archaeologists unearth unexpected find inside a tomb likely belonging to a Roman gladiator
item.description: In Turkey, archaeologists unearthed a tomb seemingly belonging to a Roman gladiator of the third century B.C., but were surprised to find the remains of 12 individuals inside.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Takeaways from an AP investigation of Russia recruiting Africans to make drones for use in Ukraine
item.description: About 200 women ages 18-22 from across Africa have been recruited to work in a factory alongside Russian vocational students assembling thousands of Iranian-designed attack drones to be launched into Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: EMMA BURROWS and LORI HINNANT, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Canadian woman charged with killing 3 people in 3 days, labeled a serial killer
item.description: A Canadian woman allegedly killed three people in separate incidents over three days in and around Toronto, authorities said last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli defense minister warns an attack on Iran would be 'lethal' and 'surprising'
item.description: JERUSALEM (AP) — Israel's defense minister warned on Wednesday that his country's retaliation for a recent Iranian missile attack will be “lethal” and “surprising,” while the Israeli military pushed ahead with a large-scale operation in northern Gaza and a ground offensive in Lebanon against Hezboll
item.dc:creator: TIA GOLDENBERG, SAMY MAGDY and WAFAA SHURAFA, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: North Korea vows to block border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures
item.description: To cope with 'confrontational hysteria' by U.S. and South Korean forces, North Korea said it will block its border with South Korea permanently and will cut off railways and roads to the country.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Zelenskyy will present his Ukraine 'victory plan' to Western leaders but Biden meeting is postponed
item.description: DUBROVNIK, Croatia (AP) — Ukraine's president on Wednesday said he intends to present his “victory plan” to Western leaders this week, but a weekend meeting to discuss it has been postponed as U.S. President Joe Biden said he had to stay home to respond to Hurricane Milton’ s landfall in Florida.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Fugitive father missing with 3 kids since 2021 filmed hiking in New Zealand
item.description: “This was a credible sighting, and police believe it was indeed Tom and his children,” a press release from New Zealand Police said.
item.dc:creator: Celeste Houmard, NewsNation

item.title: Kremlin confirms Trump sent COVID tests to Putin during pandemic
item.description: The Kremlin confirmed that former President Trump while in office sent Russian President Vladimir Putin COVID-19 testing devices during the height of the pandemic.
item.dc:creator: Laura Kelly, WGN 9

item.title: UN tribunal orders ex-official to repay $58.8 million lost in bad deals while getting perks
item.description: UNITED NATIONS (AP) — A U.N. tribunal has ordered a former high-ranking official to repay the United Nations $58.8 million lost in deals he made with a British businessman who showered him with interest-free loans, a Mercedes for his wife and perks for his sons.
item.dc:creator: EDITH M. LEDERER, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel hit with multiple terrorist attacks as Hezbollah rocket kills 2 Israelis while walking dogs
item.description: Israel was hit with another round of terrorist attacks as Hezbollah fired rockets into a northern border town, killing two, and knife-wielding moped driver attacked in Hadera, putting eight in the hospital.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Japan's new PM dissolves the lower house of parliament to set up a snap election
item.description: TOKYO (AP) — New Japanese Prime Minister Shigeru Ishiba dissolved the lower house of parliament Wednesday to set up an Oct. 27 snap election, asking people to trust the governing party's policies even as critics said the vote comes far too soon.
item.dc:creator: MARI YAMAGUCHI, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: North Korea vows to block border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — North Korea said Wednesday it will permanently block its border with South Korea and build front-line defense structures to cope with “confrontational hysteria" by South Korean and U.S.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Google loses final EU court appeal against 2.4 billion euro fine in antitrust shopping case
item.description: LONDON (AP) — Google lost its final legal challenge on Tuesday against a European Union penalty for giving its own shopping recommendations an illegal advantage over rivals in search results, ending a long-running antitrust case that came with a whopping fine.
item.dc:creator: KELVIN CHAN, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Mexican mayor murdered less than a week after taking office
item.description: Mayor Alejandro Arcos of Chilpancingo, Mexico, has been confirmed dead less than a week after being sworn into the notoriously dangerous city's highest office.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: How many are dead, missing one year since Hamas' attack on Israel?
item.description: Oct. 7 marks the first anniversary of Hamas’ attack on Israel, which prompted an Israeli invasion of Gaza.
item.dc:creator: Ashley Soriano, NewsNation

item.title: Russia jails American Stephen Hubbard over fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine
item.description: Stephen Hubbard, 72, of Michigan, has been sentenced by a Russian court Monday after being convicted of fighting as a mercenary in Ukraine.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Benjamin Netanyahu's Churchill moment comes amid multifront war against Iranian terror, expert says
item.description: Netanyahu is facing his greatest challenge as Israel’s longest-serving Prime Minister while leading the Jewish state in an existential war against the county’s Islamist terrorist enemies.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Israeli airstrikes rock southern suburbs of Beirut and cut off a key crossing into Syria
item.description: BEIRUT (AP) — Israel carried out another series of punishing airstrikes Friday, hitting suburban Beirut and cutting off the main border crossing between Lebanon and Syria for tens of thousands of people fleeing the Israeli bombardment of the Hezbollah militant group.
item.dc:creator: BASSEM MROUE, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: New MH370 project searches for answers on vanished plane
item.description: A new project is searching for answers about what happened to Malaysian Airlines Flight MH370 and its 239 passengers.
item.dc:creator: Jeff Arnold, NewsNation
